name: Cable Containment Fill Factor
formula_reference: BS EN 50085 (trunking); BS EN 50086 (conduit); CIBSE/BS 7671 Appendix C tables; manufacturer datasheets for tray/basket/ladder
implementation_note: Computes containment fill from cable count × cable outer-diameter / containment area or volume. Requires lookup of standard cable outer diameters (varies by csa, core count, insulation, and armouring) from BS 6004 / BS 5467 / BS 6724 / cable manufacturer data, plus the containment's internal cross-section. Maximum fill is rule-based by containment family (typically 35–45% for tray/basket, 40% for trunking, 45% for conduit). Inline LLM math reliably mis-reads cable outer diameters and applies the wrong containment-family rule on ~20% of representative cases. Must be tool-executed.

inputs.description: Major nominal size: tray/basket/ladder width; trunking width × depth; conduit diameter
Secondary nominal size (e.g. trunking depth); 0 if not applicable
Array of cables: [{count, csa_mm2, cores, insulation, armoured, outer_dia_mm?}]. Tool resolves outer_dia from standards tables if not provided.
Multiplier on the standard max-fill to enforce design margin (e.g. 0.9 to give 10% headroom)

current_fill_percent.description: Sum of cable cross-sections (or volumes for conduit) divided by containment cross-section

max_fill_percent.description: Standards limit applied (45% basket/tray, 40% trunking, 45% conduit)

cables_unresolved.description: Any cable spec missing outer_dia that the tool could not resolve from standards

runtime_contract.response: { ok: true, result: { current_fill_percent, max_fill_percent, compliant, headroom_percent, cables_unresolved }, error?: string }
